error.host_required: Host/IP is required for this transport
error.serial_required: Please pick or enter a serial port
error.agent_device_id_required: Provider device ID is required for the local agent

user.title: Add DESS Monitor Local
user.description: Add a single inverter, or an EyBond hub that auto-discovers several Wi-Fi dongles on one port.

device.title: Add inverter
device.description: Give this inverter a friendly name. You will pick the protocol, transport, and address on the next steps.

hub.title: Add EyBond hub
hub.description: One TCP listener accepts several EyBond dongles, auto-discovered by serial number (PN). After setup, open the hub options to assign a protocol to each discovered dongle.

data_description.host: Local interface to bind the TCP listener on (usually 0.0.0.0).
data_description.port: TCP port the dongles connect back to (default 8899).
data_description.eybond_broadcast: UDP broadcast target used to announce the local server to the dongles.
data_description.eybond_announce_ip: IP advertised to the dongles. Leave empty for auto-detect; set the host LAN IP in Docker bridge mode.
data_description.update_interval: How often to poll each enabled child inverter.

protocol.title: Choose protocol
protocol.description: Pick the command/data protocol spoken by the inverter or local data source.

transport.title: Choose transport
transport.description: Protocol: {protocol}. Pick how Home Assistant reaches it.

connection.title: Connection settings
connection.description: Protocol: {protocol}. Transport: {transport}. Enter the connection address and how often Home Assistant should poll the device.

data_description.host: Hostname or IP address of the inverter, gateway, agent, or local bind interface for EyBond.
data_description.port: TCP port. Defaults: 8899 for direct TCP/Modbus/EyBond, 8787 for the local agent.
data_description.serial_device: USB / RS232 device path. You can also type a path manually.
data_description.agent_device_id: providerDeviceId of the inverter exposed by solar-system-agent.
data_description.eybond_devaddr: RS485 slave address behind the EyBond dongle.
data_description.eybond_broadcast: UDP broadcast target used to announce the local server to the dongle.
data_description.eybond_announce_ip: Optional IP address advertised to the dongle. Leave empty for auto-detect.
data_description.update_interval: How often to poll the device. Lower values mean more frequent updates.
data_description.strict_crc: Drop Voltronic/PI18 frames with invalid CRC instead of trying to decode them.

abort.no_devices: No EyBond dongles have been discovered yet. Power on a dongle on the same network and wait for it to connect, then try again.
abort.dongle_offline: The dongle is not currently connected, so its serial number (PN) can't be captured for migration. Wait until it is online, then try again.
abort.not_eybond: This entry is not an EyBond device, so it can't be converted to a hub.
abort.rescan_started: Scanning for new dongles for about a minute. New dongles will appear under 'Manage discovered devices'.

error.host_required: Host/IP is required for this transport
error.serial_required: Please pick or enter a serial port
error.agent_device_id_required: Provider device ID is required for the local agent

rescan.title: Scan for new dongles
rescan.description: Broadcasts the discovery beacon for about a minute so new EyBond dongles on the network attach and get discovered. Already-connected dongles may briefly reconnect during the scan (the beacon can't target a single dongle). Submit to start.

migrate_hub.title: Convert to EyBond hub
migrate_hub.description: This converts the single-device entry into an EyBond hub: one listener that auto-discovers dongles by serial number (PN) and lets you add more later. The currently connected dongle becomes a configured child with its current protocol and address. Entity history is preserved. The dongle must be online now so its PN can be captured. Submit to convert and reload.

devices.title: Discovered devices
devices.description: Pick a dongle (by serial number / PN) to configure.

device_edit.title: Configure dongle {pn}
device_edit.description: Status: {status}. Last seen: {last_seen}. Enable the device and assign a protocol to start polling it.

data_description.protocol: Choose 'none' to keep the dongle discovered but not polled.
data_description.eybond_devaddr: RS485 slave address of the inverter behind this dongle (1 unless daisy-chained).
data_description.remove: Delete this dongle from discovery. If it is still online it will be re-discovered on its next heartbeat. Other fields are ignored when this is checked.

listener.title: Listener settings
listener.description: Change the TCP listener and polling interval. Saving reloads the hub.

protocol.title: Connection protocol
protocol.description: Pick the command/data protocol spoken by the inverter or local data source.

transport.title: Connection transport
transport.description: Protocol: {protocol}. Pick how this hub reaches it.

connection.title: Connection settings
connection.description: Protocol: {protocol}. Transport: {transport}. Update the connection address and polling interval.

data_description.host: Hostname or IP address of the inverter, gateway, agent, or local bind interface for EyBond.
data_description.port: TCP port. Defaults: 8899 for direct TCP/Modbus/EyBond, 8787 for the local agent.
data_description.serial_device: USB / RS232 device path. You can also type a path manually.
data_description.agent_device_id: providerDeviceId of the inverter exposed by solar-system-agent.
data_description.eybond_devaddr: RS485 slave address behind the EyBond dongle.
data_description.eybond_broadcast: UDP broadcast target used to announce the local server to the dongle.
data_description.eybond_announce_ip: Optional IP address advertised to the dongle. Leave empty for auto-detect.
data_description.update_interval: How often to poll the device. Lower values mean more frequent updates.
data_description.strict_crc: Drop Voltronic/PI18 frames with invalid CRC instead of trying to decode them.
